opp.ais_why:
• Zero Avoma meetings on file; SF Description null. Product scope has Merlin and Merlin Intake plus a broad S2P suite (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier). No customer dialogue evidence to confirm AI is driving the conversation. The two AI SKUs (Merlin + Merlin Intake) in scope provide a faint AI signal but it's not corroborated by any captured engagement.
• Qualified stage at $740K (the largest amount in chunk 4), BD_Engagement Unengaged, LastActivity 2026-03-16 ‚Äî about 2 months of dormancy. Despite the strategic deal size, the lack of meeting activity indicates early or cold engagement. Galp Energia is a Portuguese integrated energy company; the deal could be a strategic foothold if engagement activates.
• Low confidence: thin metadata, no Avoma, no description. AI in scope is the only signal. Defaulted DOWN at 4/5 due to absent engagement evidence. Would move UP only with a recorded engagement that surfaces Galp buyer interest in Merlin/Merlin Intake; would drop with continued dormancy beyond 6 months.

items.quote: Carlos Silva. is tagged Decision Maker in the Salesforce Contact Role but has never appeared on any call, email, or task in two years of records.
Who_will_approve_budget__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are all blank; no budget process has ever been documented.
The RFP promised for March 2026 was never launched; no BRD or evaluation criteria exist.
Marta Faustino, Bruno Meireles, and Tiago Frederico attended the Sep 2024 demo but have had zero recorded contact since; Alfredo Figueira is the sole active channel.
items.source: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2024 attendee list; Next_Step_History__c

metrics.narrative: No dollar or time-based impact has ever been quantified for this deal. The only pain evidence is qualitative and dates to the 3 Sep 2024 demo (~22 months ago, background only), where a Galp attendee described 'the challenge is managing the procurement platform separately from the ERP system' and workflows as 'complex and hard to maintain.' Quantifying the cost of that manual/disconnected process would be the move once evaluation resumes, but nothing since has re-confirmed or sized it.

sources: Next_Step_History__c, 28 Nov 2025 (asked to expand stakeholder knowledge)
champion.narrative: Alfredo Figueira is the sole point of contact and has been relaying status for two years, but the record shows no confirmed advocacy: no internal escalation, no introduction of new stakeholders since 2024, and no pushback against the project's deprioritization. His own 16 Jun 2026 message ('I believe it will be before end of 2028') reads as resigned rather than championing. He should be treated as a coach at best, not a champion.

competition.narrative: SAP is the incumbent stack Galp is trying to displace; SAP Ariba, JAGGAER, and iValua are named alternatives in Competitors__c from the original 2024 RFI stage. None are currently active competitors in a live bake-off because Galp has frozen the entire replacement decision, not just Zycus's bid, since March 2026. The wedge (Merlin/AI-driven S2P plus contract automation) remains intact for whenever the freeze lifts.

identify_pain.narrative: Real, specific operational pain was articulated during the 3 Sep 2024 demo: manual GL-account mapping from material/service codes, a procurement platform disconnected from the ERP, and complex, hard-to-maintain approval workflows. This pain was confirmed by Galp attendees but has not been re-raised or re-confirmed in the roughly 22 months since, and the pain owner was never named beyond the generic attendee group.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting activity has ever occurred; the deal has never progressed past informal discovery and one demo. There is no MSA, SOW, or procurement-channel discussion on record, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, so no forcing function exists either.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has ever engaged directly. Carlos Silva. carries a Decision Maker Contact Role tagged at Galp Energia, but the record shows him as a partner-channel contact who has never appeared on a single call, email, or task in two years. Budget approval fields (Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) are all blank. The entire relationship runs through Alfredo Figueira, an IT/procurement contact with no stated budget authority.

decision_process.narrative: The only visible process was a planned RFP for March 2026, confirmed twice by Alfredo Figueira (Dec 2025), then shelved before it started. No stakeholder sequence, approval chain, or evaluation committee has ever been described beyond the single contact relaying status updates.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria or BRD exists. Alfredo Figueira confirmed on 11 Dec 2025 that 'decisions finally were made and we will start the RFP march 2026,' which would have produced criteria, but the RFP never launched: on 16 Mar 2026 he reported the project suspended for the Moev merger instead.

items.name: Alfredo confirms long-term suspension
items.summary: Replying to Pierre's direct question about whether the project could be relaunched in 2026 or would slip further, Alfredo states he has no concrete plan yet but expects it won't move before end of 2028 — effectively confirming a multi-year freeze tied to the MOEV strategic partnership.

day_summary.overall: Pierre finally got a straight answer from Alfredo on the deal's fate after months of it being stuck behind Galp's MOEV partnership: the project isn't dead, but it's shelved with no relaunch expected before the end of 2028. This confirms the deal is effectively frozen for the foreseeable future — nothing to chase here, just a marker to revisit in roughly two years.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — No live competitive bake-off exists today: the entire evaluation (SAP incumbent replacement versus SAP Ariba, JAGGAER, and iValua) has been frozen since Galp suspended the project on 16 Mar 2026. SAP remains the single strongest 'current threat' only in the passive sense that it is the status quo Zycus must eventually displace once Galp resumes the decision; none of the named alternatives are actively being evaluated.

Quantifying the cost of that manual/disconnected process would be the move once evaluation resumes, but nothing since has re-confirmed or sized it.

blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No live competitive bake-off exists today: the entire evaluation (SAP incumbent replacement versus SAP Ariba, JAGGAER, and iValua) has been frozen since Galp suspended the project on 16 Mar 2026.

bullets.text: No budget process or funding authority has ever been confirmed for this project.
No economic buyer engaged; single point of contact carries the entire relationship.
Close date has slipped seven times over more than two years and now sits at 16 Mar 2027 with no supporting evidence.

items: Ask Alfredo Figueira to name who now owns the post-Moev procurement roadmap decision. (by 17 Jun)
Ask the EY partner contact to independently confirm Galp's post-Moev procurement priorities. (by 22 Jun)
Re-engage Marta Faustino, Bruno Meireles, and Tiago Frederico with a short check-in. (by 25 Jun)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 12 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 6 is carried by business case strength +1.00 (weight 10) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'business case' in…; differentiation strength +0.30 (weight 20). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 46 (roughly flat): rep confidence 10% (low). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.75).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 40 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 5 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (6), nudged by commitment 40 and momentum 46, and dragged by risk 12. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: business case strength +1.00 (weight 10) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'business case' in Next-Step/narrative
buyer touch 22d ago — within the ~30d cadence this stage needs

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Why__c flags zero recent Avoma meetings; this is a hygiene gap since a substantive 3-hour demo did occur on 3 Sep 2024 (background), where Galp attendees engaged seriously with Merlin Assist and asked pointed questions about AI hallucination risk. No AI conversation has occurred since, consistent with the AIS default-down read.

business_case.evidence: No ROI or business case has been quantified or discussed in the last 60 days; the project itself remains suspended.

items.change: StageName regressed from Formal Evaluation back to Qualified
Opportunity owner changed from Renaud Vadon to Pierre Meraud
Alfredo Figueira confirmed the RFP would start March 2026
Buyer confirmed the project is suspended due to a potential Moev merger; CloseDate pushed to 2027-03-16 (7th slip)
Alfredo Figueira stated he has no restart visibility and personally guesses a delay to around 2028

deal_movement.summary: A long, repeatedly-slipped nurture deal that stalled at Qualified after a strong 2024 demo, was overtaken in March 2026 by Galp's Moev merger, and on 16 Jun 2026 the buyer signaled the freeze could last years longer than the current close date implies.

items: No new stakeholder contact since the Sep 2024 demo
Buyer named a ~2028 guess for restart, not a plan
Two years of repeated 'still evaluating internally' messages with no forward movement
confidence_signals.summary: The deal has cooled from a strong technical demo in 2024 to an indefinite hold with a discouraging buyer timeline guess; no counter-signal of urgency exists.

north_star_verdict.math: Currently sitting in Qualified since 24 Feb 2025 (~17 months in stage after regressing back from Formal Evaluation, reached only briefly in Sep-Dec 2024). Close date has slipped seven times over two years, most recently from 2026-09-30 to 2027-03-16 on 16 Mar 2026 (today, 8 Jul 2026, is 251 days out from that date). Against the buyer's own stated guess ('before end of 2028'), the current close date is understated by roughly 18-21 months.
evidence: Task email 16 Jun 2026, Alfredo Figueira: 'don't worry, at the moment I don't have any idea but I believe it will be before end of 2028.'
Next_Step__c entry 16 Mar 2026: project suspended due to a potential Moev merger.
OpportunityFieldHistory: CloseDate 2026-09-30 -> 2027-03-16 on 2026-03-16, the 7th slip since the first push on 2024-10-18.
north_star_verdict.headline: Galp's Source-to-Pay evaluation has been suspended since 16 Mar 2026 following Galp's strategic partnership with Moev, and on 16 Jun 2026 (~3 weeks ago) the sole buyer contact told Pierre Meraud he has no visibility on restart timing and personally expects a delay to around 2028.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Keep as Pipeline with no committed close date; treat 2027-03-16 as a placeholder until Galp confirms a concrete post-Moev restart timeline.

flags: No economic buyer named after 24 months; use the promised September 2026 update to ask directly who signs off for the merged entity.
The RFP keeps slipping; propose a lighter phased start (e.g. eProcurement/eInvoicing first) instead of waiting on a full RFP relaunch.
Multi-thread beyond Alfredo Figueira now: reach Tânia Madeira or Bruno Meireles directly rather than waiting on his sole reply.
Use this pause to refresh the ROI and Merlin/I2O business case so it is ready the moment Galp resumes evaluation post-merger.
Push to confirm who at Galp actually controls procurement-platform budget; two years in, no economic buyer has ever been named.
Single-threaded through Alfredo Figueira for over a year with no economic buyer ever engaged; surfacing a budget owner inside the merged Galp-MOEV entity is the prerequisite to moving past Qualified.

Galp's own compelling event, low adoption of its existing Jaggaer and SAP procurement tools, is the strongest wedge on record; keep the value story anchored to that documented pain rather than a generic AI pitch so it survives the merger distraction.

The internal 1-July-2026 checkpoint to reassess this opportunity as Closed Lost has already passed without action; resolve that now so the record reflects the deal's true state rather than sitting indefinitely in Pipeline.

Convert the 2024 demo's strong technical engagement (Merlin Assist, GenAI reliability, contract automation) into a refreshed AI/ROI narrative to stay relevant once the freeze lifts, rather than letting the account go fully cold.
best_practice_check.summary: Two years in, the deal survives only because Alfredo Figueira keeps replying; the highest-leverage moves are widening the thread and getting an independent read on Galp's real priorities.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer's only recent input (16 Jun 2026) was a vague personal timeline guess with no comparison to any competitor.

competitive_position.summary: No live competitive bake-off exists today: the entire evaluation (SAP incumbent replacement versus SAP Ariba, JAGGAER, and iValua) has been frozen since Galp suspended the project on 16 Mar 2026. SAP remains the single strongest 'current threat' only in the passive sense that it is the status quo Zycus must eventually displace once Galp resumes the decision; none of the named alternatives are actively being evaluated.

competitors.name: SAP (current stack)
competitors.quote: The challenge is managing the procurement platform separately from the ERP system.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus's S2P suite plus Merlin AI as the faster, unified replacement once evaluation resumes.

competitors.name: JAGGAER (current stack)
competitors.quote: Named alongside the current environment as a considered alternative.
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize breadth of the S2P suite (source-to-pay plus risk) versus a narrower footprint.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Named as a considered alternative platform during the original RFI stage.
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on AI-native intake and contract automation not native to Ariba.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: Named as a considered alternative platform during the original RFI stage.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the Merlin Intake / GenAI story that generated strong buyer questions in the 2024 demo.

competitors.name: SAP / SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Low adoption of existing solutions (Jaegger and SAP).
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus's integrated S2P suite against SAP's fragmented procurement modules and low internal adoption; lead with ease of use and Merlin Assist once the buyer re-engages.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Low adoption of existing solutions (Jaegger and SAP).
competitors.how_we_win: Use Jaggaer's cited low adoption as the direct wedge for a simpler, AI-assisted user experience.
